item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as across multiple sources there has been no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: Wiki-Walk Suggestion
item.content:encoded:
As MTF ε-7 is on the Task Forces page:

Mobile Task Force Epsilon-7 ("Forget-Me-Nots") is to continuously monitor and disrupt civilian development of mnestics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite dig this one. Creative containment in order to outsmart the conventionally unstoppable is one of my favorite tropes.

item.title: The Paladin and the Pawn belong to us.
item.content:encoded: Very cryptic.

item.title: Re: so how exactly does this been an "anti-memetic"?
item.content:encoded:
Yes, precisely.

The idea of the original trees was hazardous information so CD made them antimemetic so that people actively will ignore them without perceiving or remembering them.

item.title: so how exactly does this been an "anti-memetic"?
item.content:encoded: I mean, beside been an information, these trees doesn't spread or repeat acts themselves.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I've PMed the author about it. They can make the call.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Well you put the comment immediately after "mnestic" with no space, so it would be extra clueless to miss it. I haven't had this problem since I put the comments in.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: That's provided one reads the comment before editing the article.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Instead of locking it, what about just putting in a comment that it isn't a typo (as I have done with my uses of the term)?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: No worries. Given that it's happened before, it was about time it got addressed properly.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: My mistake, in this case. An internet search led me to believe "mnestic" was merely a suffix and not a word in itself, and I erred on the side of thinking it was a typo.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Reverted, since it's meant to be "mnestics" without the a. See this post on the previous discussion page: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1228723/scp-2358#post-2351297

Also, given that this is the second time this has happened, I'm going to lock the page. Readers, please make a post in the discussion thread if there are any changes you think should be made!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed a minor typo in the second paragraph.

"mnestics" to "amnestics"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you mean Fantem, and it is the other way around, SCP-2999 predates this by quite a bit. I wouldn't take that to mean this actually directly relates to Pitch Haven, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't Famine reference "the paladin and the pawn belong to us" in SCP-2999?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny story…
So I read this one about a year ago. I was just scrolling down through the Series III articles looking for one with an interesting name, and saw this one called, "I've seen this somewhere before…". So I read it… and recall that I did in fact already read this.

I said to myself "I've seen this somewhere before…" not realizing the sheer irony of the situation.

But good job anyway. I have a hard time latching on to this one, but others like it, and you put a lot of effort into it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speaking of that… the trouble with using weird characters is that some phones read them as emoji :-( http://imgur.com/tKvz5sE I don't think it's supposed to look like this :-X

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely nothing.

It just sounds cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

A secret message in the memetic kill agent thing

the paladin and the pawn belong to us

What does it mean???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's little problem.

The three known instances of SCP-2358 exist in the Mongkok district, Hong Kong…..

Mongkok district(旺角區) is not a district where existed in Hong Kong today.

It was combined with nearby district and reform into a new district call Yau Tsim Mong District(油尖旺區) in 1994.

Since "Mongkok district, Hong Kong"is not exist today, renamed to "Yau Tsim Mong District, Hong Kong" or just "Mongkok, Hong Kong" is better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an okay concept (dangerous meme that requires an antimeme filter in order to be contained) with shoddy execution. I didn't much care for Clarissa explainin' it all in the final note there. Ultimately I guess I just didn't understand what the payoff for this was supposed to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I was ready to downvote when I saw that it was yet another long-ish article with a nice note explaining everything at the end so the author could wrap it up all nice and neat without much in the way of pacing. Then, when I saw it was memetic fuckery that required a convoluted explanation for likely inadequate payoff, I was ready to double-downvote.

Then I read to around halfway through the first note, and was actually kind of intrigued, perhaps even ready for an upvote. Then I got to the final note, and the drawn out, boring-ass explanation and saw my suspicions were confirmed. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Between this and those things that I think existed, seems that I'll have to particularly polish a certain something, if I ever get working on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did mean to put "mnestics" with an "m".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the record, mnestics is not a typo. It's the opposite of amnestics, in a sense. Its usage here warrants mnesitcs. If you're not sure, ask the author, as I'm 99.999% sure Fae meant it like mnestics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Correct me if I'm wrong, but,
amnestics are the things that get rid of memories and mnestics do… about the opposite, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was more me complimenting the other piece and looking for a cool phrase rather than crosslinking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*shrugs*

I wasn't told how it fits in, so I can't really say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow how did I miss that? Authors, is this just a shoutout or has Clovis has infiltrated the CD?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a reference to another great piece in a great canon. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did anyone else bother looking at the letters in the cognitohazard?

+
-

the palladin and the pawn belong to us

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, sorry about the misunderstanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed "keter" tag, because this skip isn't actually Keter. Look at SCP-1451, which uses a similar "SCP document from some other mysterious place" trope, where the Euclid object is actually implied to be Keter… but it's not Keter in-universe. It's also not like SCP-1500, where the bait-and-switch of Safe to Keter allows for both a Keter and a Safe tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad there was the whole antimeme addendum angle to this, or it wouldn't have been as interesting. That said, I feel like it's not doing anything new with the concept, which I've already seen presented quite well in tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that you've seen the tree in person means that you're going to die soon anyway, but the amnestics should be able to buy you a few extra hours.

This might be scarier if you removed the "in person" and implied that the artists depiction was hazardous and the reader is in danger.

Just a thought.
Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) MTF added.

2) I won't add anymore here, but I mat develop them in more pieces later on.

3) In-universe, the writers alread had the phrasing in their mind after reading the original document, so that influenced them in a non-anomalous but still memetic way. Out-of-universe, it's meant to reflect a trend that Antimemetics seems to include a lot of going in circles and events playing out over and over and over and over…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT On a second read I've removed my downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How do you know that the artist didn't use photoshop? Also why would you expect any specially high level of artistic skill from a convicted mass murderer or North Korean political prisoner or whatever. If anything the image is too good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted because the image is too obviously photoshopped for me to take it seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks killerax!

I don't really have a constructive alternative, sadly.

Non-constructive opinion.
– hide block

I think I find it frustrating because, in my headcanon, mnestics are super rare and not well known even within the Foundation, and that civilians developing them is either many decades off or something that is being handled on a much higher covert level than a single containment procedure.


Actually, now that I've written that… I'd be more comfortable with that line if you made up a project name or MTF whose job that was. Just something to indicate that it's an ongoing, existing effort.

As for the rest, thanks for your response.

The aftereffects make sense for the context, they just don't stand out.

As far as the CC division, then, I would be more interested if there was some indication of what happened to them. I understand why that statement is contradictory and unrealistic, but given that (For me) they're the driving force here then I would like to see more of them.

Edit: Also, the exact text replication on the effects is a little odd, unless the current procedure is actually a fragment of the old documentation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The bird on the cereal box is SCP-1160 btw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) Huh, hadn't seen 1008 before, interesting read. The effects were mostly just my expounding on the concept of what happens after an idea is "planted" in your head.

2) Bird on the cereal box?

3) For the same reason that there's no records of the CC division, they were dealing with trying to contain several antimemes, many of which have detrimental effects on information.

4) Nice to see another anitmemetics fan. I'm sorry that the procedures frustrated you, I had tried to form the most logical set that I could. Do you have an alternate suggestion for what I might try in the containment procedures?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just not buying this one.

As others have said, the outcomes of the memetic effects (If not the mechanism) are pretty boring and nothing new. Put me in mind of SCP-1008, actually.

The object-which-must-must-be-perceived-or-grows was more interesting as the bird on the cereal box.

Ultimately, similar to sirpudding's comment, the only interesting thing about this is the prior containment and its aftereffects, and they just don't stand out for me. Particularly, why did the Foundation forget the prior containment? The anti-meme keeps people from storing perceptions of the trees, not secondary information about them, or neither the current nor the past documentation would be here.

Also, I love antimemetics. I loved 055, I loved the A-M department article that started the recent resurgence, and i love the extinct horizon guardians. But I feel like if I see another article cautioning against civilian mnestics in the containment procedures, my head is going to explode.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough, though the core concept of how antimemetics are in use here bothers me enough to keep a downvote regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interesting thing here isn't the anomaly itself, it is what the Counterconceptual Division did to contain it, and what it now looks like to the Foundation at large as a result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, but it still doesn't do anything special; then it's just another memetic thing what kills you, and doesn't have that something that the other antimemetic skips or tales had to make it unique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cute Pitch Haven reference, by the way.

item.title: Re: Authorative thingamajig.
item.content:encoded: Ooh.. Dashed cunning.

item.title: Re: Authorative thingamajig.
item.content:encoded: Look closely. Same initials, different names.

item.title: Re: Authorative thingamajig.
item.content:encoded:
Yet they have the same department head. I wonder if she remembers both of them…

Anyway, this is the best one (after 55). I love it when I have to make a deduction to figure out a thing. Have all my upvotes (1 memetic and the set of uncountable sets antimemetic upvotes).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^ Listen to this guy.

item.title: Authorative thingamajig.
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to Delliott and Goomy for looking this over. Special thanks to qntm for inspiring and reviewing this.

Explanation.
You saw nothing.

The antimemetic part of the anomaly is that it's impossible to see and remember seeing the trees without a dose of mnestics in your system. This effect was caused by the antimemetic perception filter that the CC division applied. The weird "see trees everywhere" effect was the original effect from before they were hidden. The filter doesn't apply to painting of the trees (which is why there can be an image, they had an artistic D-Class do a painting after seeing the tree) but does apply to photos.

The Counterconceptual Division is a bit of mischief qntm and I cooked up. They serve the same basic purpose that the Antimemetic Department serves. Why do two divisions exist that do the same job? They can't remember each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From what I understand, the general gist of it is this; the trees started out as memetic anomalies that would rapidly grow when not observed by humans, but when observed by humans would shrink and at the same time infiltrate the human's brain, doing its memetic magic in their mind and killing them. The implication is that the trees purposely grew in certain ways to maximize the amount of humans that saw it, so it could take root in as many minds as possible. Then someone working at the antimemetics department managed to attach some sort of filter/antimeme/antimemetic filter to the trees, making it so humans could still sorta perceive it- enough to the point where the tree's growth is halted but not enough so that the trees could infiltrate the person's mind. The Foundation worker moved the trees to corwded areas so their growth would be constantly held in check, some antimemetic stuff happened to the worker who did it, and now the Foundation doesn't remember the truth behind the trees, and so they just leave them be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure what's going on here. I also think this is stretching the "antimeme" kinda far, as each skip or tale involving the use of antimemes prior to this either equate to not being able to remember something at all, or destroying information. This… seems to do neither? It just sends people into a catatonic state if they perceive it, and the letters afterward are gibberish to me.

Also, wouldn't this image affect you since you're able to perceive it? Or does it not extend to drawings of it? How can you even draw it if it's an antimeme?
